channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Designing Lattice Spin Models and Magnon Gaps with Supercurrents
item.description:
Author(s): Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder

Electric control over magnetic interactions at the level of individual spins is relevant for a variety of quantum applications, such as qubits, memory, and sensor functionality. We show here that spin lattices and magnon gaps can be controlled with a supercurrent. Remarkably, a spin-polarized superc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder

Electric control over magnetic interactions at the level of individual spins is relevant for a variety of quantum applications, such as qubits, memory, and sensor functionality. We show here that spin lattices and magnon gaps can be controlled with a supercurrent. Remarkably, a spin-polarized superc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Designing Lattice Spin Models and Magnon Gaps with Supercurrents
item.dc:creator: Johanne Bratland Tjernshaugen, Martin Tang Bruland, and Jacob Linder
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.description:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a $d$-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermag…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing

Altermagnets hold great potential for spintronic applications, yet their intrinsic spin dynamics and associated transport properties remain largely unexplored. Here, we investigate spin-resolved quantum transport in a multiterminal setup based on a d-wave altermagnet. It is found that the altermagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106301] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic Spin Precession and Spin Transistor
item.dc:creator: Li-Shuo Liu, Kai Shao, Hai-Dong Li, Xiangang Wan, Wei Chen, and D. Y. Xing
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Type-II Antiferroelectricity
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Wang, Zhi-Ming Yu, Chaoxi Cui, Yilin Han, Tingli He, Weikang Wu, Run-Wu Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, and Yugui Yao

Antiferroelectricity is a fundamental concept in physics and materials science. Conventional antiferroelectrics (AFEs) have the picture of alternating local electric dipoles defined in real space. Here, we discover a new class of AFEs, termed type-II AFEs, which possess opposite polarizations define…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106402] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Wang, Zhi-Ming Yu, Chaoxi Cui, Yilin Han, Tingli He, Weikang Wu, Run-Wu Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, and Yugui Yao

Antiferroelectricity is a fundamental concept in physics and materials science. Conventional antiferroelectrics (AFEs) have the picture of alternating local electric dipoles defined in real space. Here, we discover a new class of AFEs, termed type-II AFEs, which possess opposite polarizations define…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106402] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Type-II Antiferroelectricity
item.dc:creator: Yang Wang, Zhi-Ming Yu, Chaoxi Cui, Yilin Han, Tingli He, Weikang Wu, Run-Wu Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, and Yugui Yao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Miniband Generation by Surface Acoustic Waves
item.description:
Author(s): Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer

We introduce a new class of tunable periodic structures formed by launching two obliquely propagating surface acoustic waves on a piezoelectric substrate that supports a two-dimensional material. The resulting acoustoelectric superlattice exhibits two salient features. First, its periodicity is wide…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106403] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer

We introduce a new class of tunable periodic structures formed by launching two obliquely propagating surface acoustic waves on a piezoelectric substrate that supports a two-dimensional material. The resulting acoustoelectric superlattice exhibits two salient features. First, its periodicity is wide…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106403] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Miniband Generation by Surface Acoustic Waves
item.dc:creator: Eli Meril, Unmesh Ghorai, Tobias Holder, and Rafi Bistritzer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Kekulé Order from Diffuse Nesting near Higher-Order Van Hove Points
item.description:
Author(s): Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann

Translation symmetry-breaking order is assumed to be suppressed by the lack of Fermi surface nesting near certain higher-order Van Hove singularities (HOVHS). We show that the anisotropic band-flattening inherent to such HOVHS, combined with broadening of the Fermi surface due to elevated critical s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106503] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann

Translation symmetry-breaking order is assumed to be suppressed by the lack of Fermi surface nesting near certain higher-order Van Hove singularities (HOVHS). We show that the anisotropic band-flattening inherent to such HOVHS, combined with broadening of the Fermi surface due to elevated critical s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106503] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Kekulé Order from Diffuse Nesting near Higher-Order Van Hove Points
item.dc:creator: Jonas Beck, Jonathan Bodky, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ronny Thomale, Julian Ingham, Lennart Klebl, and Hendrik Hohmann
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.description:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors ${\text{FeSe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Ch}}_{x}$ ($\mathrm{Ch}=\mathrm{S},\mathrm{Te}$) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao

Iron chalcogenide superconductors FeSe1−xChx (Ch=S,Te) exhibit an unusual double-dome superconducting phase diagram, the microscopic origin of which remains unclear. Here, we use inelastic neutron scattering to probe spin excitations in single-crystalline FeSe0.67Te0.33, positioned at the supercondu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106504] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Dual Spin Excitation Components in ${\mathrm{FeSe}}_{0.67}{\mathrm{Te}}_{0.33}$
item.dc:creator: Hao Zhang, Hongliang Wo, Yimeng Gu, Zeyu Kao, Gaofeng Ding, Kazuki Iida, Kazuhiko Ikeuchi, and Jun Zhao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.description:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei

Equilibrium interlayer exciton condensation is common in bilayer quantum Hall systems and is characterized by spontaneous phase coherence between isolated layers. It has been predicted that similar physics can occur in the absence of a magnetic field in some two-dimensional semiconductor bilayers. I…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106602] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological Excitonic Insulators in Electron Bilayers Modulated by Twisted Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.dc:creator: Yongxin Zeng, Allan H. MacDonald, and Nemin Wei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Competing Magnetic Anisotropy and Domain-Wall Density for Optimizing Magnetization-Induced Water-Oxidation Enhancement
item.description:
Author(s): Anke Yu, Durgesh Kumar, Zizhao Gong, Leonhard Tannesia, Hasibur Rahaman, Pengfei Song, Tianze Wu, Ramu Maddu, Pinkesh Kumar Mishra, Xiao Renshaw Wang, S. N. Piramanayagam, and Zhichuan J. Xu

Optimal spin-related enhancement of oxygen evolution reaction activity is shown to emerge from the balance between perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and the density of magnetic domain walls.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 108001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anke Yu, Durgesh Kumar, Zizhao Gong, Leonhard Tannesia, Hasibur Rahaman, Pengfei Song, Tianze Wu, Ramu Maddu, Pinkesh Kumar Mishra, Xiao Renshaw Wang, S. N. Piramanayagam, and Zhichuan J. Xu

Optimal spin-related enhancement of oxygen evolution reaction activity is shown to emerge from the balance between perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and the density of magnetic domain walls.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 108001] Published Wed Mar 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Competing Magnetic Anisotropy and Domain-Wall Density for Optimizing Magnetization-Induced Water-Oxidation Enhancement
item.dc:creator: Anke Yu, Durgesh Kumar, Zizhao Gong, Leonhard Tannesia, Hasibur Rahaman, Pengfei Song, Tianze Wu, Ramu Maddu, Pinkesh Kumar Mishra, Xiao Renshaw Wang, S. N. Piramanayagam, and Zhichuan J. Xu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics
item.prism:section: Polymers, Chemical Physics, Soft Matter, and Biological Physics

item.title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.description:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

A unified theory explains the recent discovery of superconductivity and reentrant integer quantum anomalous Hall states in twisted MoTe2 in terms of the disorder-broadened Landau-Hofstadter bands of doped anyons.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106501] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators
item.dc:creator: Pavel A. Nosov, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

High-resolution electron scattering confirms the dimensional transition of exciton dispersion from parabolic (3D) to massless linear (2D) behavior near the Brillouin zone center.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106502] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dimensionality-Dependent Exciton Dispersion in a Single-Band Mott Insulator
item.dc:creator: Zhibin Su, Junjian Mi, Shaohua Yan, Jiade Li, Siwei Xue, Zhiyu Tao, Enling Wang, Xiongfei Shi, Hechang Lei, Zhuan Xu, Jiandong Guo, and Xuetao Zhu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

Dirac semimetals provide a new platform for the quantum Hall effect at low magnetic fields. In the presence of strong spin-orbit coupling, a spin-split Landau level is expected to enhance the bulk quasiparticle excitation. Here we report NMR spectroscopy that site selectively probes dynamic spin sus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106601] Published Tue Mar 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Condensed Spin Excitation of Quantized Dirac Fermions in the Quasi-Two-Dimensional Semimetal ${\mathrm{BaMnBi}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Masashi Kumazaki, Azimjon Temurjonov, Takaaki Jinno, Yukihiro Watanabe, Taku Matsuhita, Yoshiaki Kobayashi, and Yasuhiro Shimizu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Probing Coherences and Itinerant Magnetism in a Dipolar Lattice Gas
item.description:
Author(s): Thomas Lauprêtre, Jose Daniel Bernal, Youcef Baamara, Ana Maria Rey, Laurent Vernac, and Bruno Laburthe-Tolra

We report on the study of itinerant magnetism of lattice-trapped magnetic atoms, driven by magnetic dipole-dipole interactions, in the low-entropy and close-to-unit filling regime. We have used advanced dynamical decoupling techniques to efficiently suppress the sensitivity to magnetic field fluctua…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Thomas Lauprêtre, Jose Daniel Bernal, Youcef Baamara, Ana Maria Rey, Laurent Vernac, and Bruno Laburthe-Tolra

We report on the study of itinerant magnetism of lattice-trapped magnetic atoms, driven by magnetic dipole-dipole interactions, in the low-entropy and close-to-unit filling regime. We have used advanced dynamical decoupling techniques to efficiently suppress the sensitivity to magnetic field fluctua…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Probing Coherences and Itinerant Magnetism in a Dipolar Lattice Gas
item.dc:creator: Thomas Lauprêtre, Jose Daniel Bernal, Youcef Baamara, Ana Maria Rey, Laurent Vernac, and Bruno Laburthe-Tolra

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Photonic Cyclic Orbit Bound to a Single Weyl Point
item.description:
Author(s): Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

Weyl orbits are topologically protected cyclotron trajectories that connect Weyl nodes of opposite chiralities in momentum space, coupling bulk chiral Landau levels and surface Fermi arcs to produce quantum oscillations under static magnetic fields. While well-studied in condensed matter systems, th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103801] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

Weyl orbits are topologically protected cyclotron trajectories that connect Weyl nodes of opposite chiralities in momentum space, coupling bulk chiral Landau levels and surface Fermi arcs to produce quantum oscillations under static magnetic fields. While well-studied in condensed matter systems, th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 103801] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Photonic Cyclic Orbit Bound to a Single Weyl Point
item.dc:creator: Zhongfu Li, Qingyang Mo, Oubo You, Qingdong Yang, Shaojie Ma, Xinhua Wen, Yuanjiang Xiang, and Shuang Zhang

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Observation of Quasisteady Dark Excitons and Gapped State in a Doped Semiconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

Excitons underpin optical phenomena and can intertwine with correlated electronic states. Unlike bright excitons, dark excitons evade conventional optical detection, and their impact on quasiequilibrium electronic structures remains largely unexplored. Here, using angle-resolved photoemission spectr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

Excitons underpin optical phenomena and can intertwine with correlated electronic states. Unlike bright excitons, dark excitons evade conventional optical detection, and their impact on quasiequilibrium electronic structures remains largely unexplored. Here, using angle-resolved photoemission spectr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106401] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Observation of Quasisteady Dark Excitons and Gapped State in a Doped Semiconductor
item.dc:creator: Shangkun Mo, Yunfei Bai, Chunlong Wu, Xingxia Cui, Guangqiang Mei, Qiang Wan, Renzhe Li, Cao Peng, Keming Zhao, Dingkun Qin, Shuming Yu, Hao Zhong, Xingzhe Wang, Enting Li, Yiwei Li, Limin Cao, Min Feng, Sheng Meng, and Nan Xu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Disentangling Electronic and Ionic Nonlinear Polarization Effects in Bulk THz Kerr Response
item.description:
Author(s): Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

Terahertz (THz) spectroscopy is a powerful probe of low-energy excitations in complex materials. Extending it into the nonlinear regime broadens its scope and can provide valuable insight into interactions among these modes. However, interpreting nonlinear spectra is challenging because resonant fea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106901] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

Terahertz (THz) spectroscopy is a powerful probe of low-energy excitations in complex materials. Extending it into the nonlinear regime broadens its scope and can provide valuable insight into interactions among these modes. However, interpreting nonlinear spectra is challenging because resonant fea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106901] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Disentangling Electronic and Ionic Nonlinear Polarization Effects in Bulk THz Kerr Response
item.dc:creator: Chao Shen, Maximilian Frenzel, Sebastian F. Maehrlein, and Zhanybek Alpichshev

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Giant Helicity-Dependent Second Harmonic Generation in Type-2 Weyl Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

Second harmonic generation (SHG) has emerged as a powerful probe of fundamental geometric and topological attributes of the band structure of the Weyl semimetals (WSMs) through the photocurrent generation (shift, injection, and anomalous currents). Notably, circular photocurrents, such as injection …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106902] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

Second harmonic generation (SHG) has emerged as a powerful probe of fundamental geometric and topological attributes of the band structure of the Weyl semimetals (WSMs) through the photocurrent generation (shift, injection, and anomalous currents). Notably, circular photocurrents, such as injection …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 106902] Published Mon Mar 09, 2026
item.dc:title: Giant Helicity-Dependent Second Harmonic Generation in Type-2 Weyl Semimetal
item.dc:creator: Aindrila Sinha, K. P. Mithun, D. V. S. Muthu, and A. K. Sood

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui

Bilayer WSe2 hosts bright interlayer A and B excitons even when separated by ~300 meV from intralayer excitons, a regime where conventional exciton hybridization models fail.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096903] Published Fri Mar 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Brightening Interlayer Excitons by Electric-Field-Driven Hole Transfer in Bilayer ${\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Tianyi Ouyang, Erfu Liu, Soonyoung Cha, Rukai Cang, Raj Kumar Paudel, Yiyang Sun, Zhaoran Xu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Nathaniel M. Gabor, Yia-Chung Chang, and Chun Hung Lui
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Mpemba Effect in Long-Range Spin Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares

One of the manifestations of the quantum Mpemba effect (QME) is that a tilted ferromagnet exhibits faster restoration of the spin-rotational symmetry after a quantum quench when starting from a larger tilt angle. This phenomenon has recently been observed experimentally in an ion trap that simulates…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares

One of the manifestations of the quantum Mpemba effect (QME) is that a tilted ferromagnet exhibits faster restoration of the spin-rotational symmetry after a quantum quench when starting from a larger tilt angle. This phenomenon has recently been observed experimentally in an ion trap that simulates…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 090402] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Mpemba Effect in Long-Range Spin Systems
item.dc:creator: Shion Yamashika and Filiberto Ares
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Driven-Dissipative Landau Polaritons: Two Highly Nonlinearly Coupled Quantum Harmonic Oscillators
item.description:
Author(s): Farokh Mivehvar

Landau levels (LLs) are the massively degenerate discrete energy spectra of charged particles in a transverse magnetic field, and they lie at the heart of many intriguing phenomena, such as the integer and fractional quantum Hall effects as well as quantized vortices. In this Letter, we consider cou…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093602] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Farokh Mivehvar

Landau levels (LLs) are the massively degenerate discrete energy spectra of charged particles in a transverse magnetic field, and they lie at the heart of many intriguing phenomena, such as the integer and fractional quantum Hall effects as well as quantized vortices. In this Letter, we consider cou…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093602] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Driven-Dissipative Landau Polaritons: Two Highly Nonlinearly Coupled Quantum Harmonic Oscillators
item.dc:creator: Farokh Mivehvar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic $t–{t}^{′}–J$ model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level $δ$ and hopping ratio ${t}^{′}/t$, …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng

Inspired by the recent experimental advances in cold atom quantum simulators, we explore the experimentally implemented bosonic t–t′–J model on the square lattice using large-scale density matrix renormalization group simulations. By tuning the doping level δ and hopping ratio t′/t, we uncover six d…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096506] Published Thu Mar 05, 2026
item.dc:title: Competing and Intertwined Orders in Boson-Doped Mott Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Xin Lu, Jia-Xin Zhang, Lukas Homeier, Shou-Shu Gong, D. N. Sheng, and Zheng-Yu Weng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Beyond Mean-Field Dynamics of the Dicke Model with Non-Markovian Dephasing
item.description:
Author(s): Anqi Mu, Nathan Ng, Andrew J. Millis, and David R. Reichman

We present a density matrix based time dependent projection operator formalism to calculate the beyond mean-field dynamics of systems with non-Markovian local baths and one-to-all interactions. Such models encapsulate the physics of condensed phase systems immersed in optical cavities. We use this m…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093601] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anqi Mu, Nathan Ng, Andrew J. Millis, and David R. Reichman

We present a density matrix based time dependent projection operator formalism to calculate the beyond mean-field dynamics of systems with non-Markovian local baths and one-to-all interactions. Such models encapsulate the physics of condensed phase systems immersed in optical cavities. We use this m…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 093601] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Beyond Mean-Field Dynamics of the Dicke Model with Non-Markovian Dephasing
item.dc:creator: Anqi Mu, Nathan Ng, Andrew J. Millis, and David R. Reichman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.description:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu

The current phase relation (CPR) determines the electromagnetic response of Josephson junctions in superconducting circuits. It is sensitive to the weak link materials and interface quality. In this work, we report the intrinsic Josephson effect originating from domains in the single-crystal kagome …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096301] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic-Flux Tuning of Second Harmonic Content in Intrinsic ${\mathrm{CsV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ Josephson Junction
item.dc:creator: Jing-Jing Chen, Han-Xin Lou, Xing-Guo Ye, Zhen-Bing Tan, An-Qi Wang, Zhen-Tao Zhang, Yu-Xuan Wang, Xin Liao, Zhen-Sheng Zhang, Zhi-Min Liao, and Da-Peng Yu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Localization Transition for Interacting Quantum Particles in Colored-Noise Disorder
item.description:
Author(s): Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi

We investigate the localization transition of interacting particles in a one-dimensional system with colored-noise disorder, where backward scattering processes are suppressed beyond a cutoff. Employing two complementary renormalization group procedures, we derive the phase diagram and reveal a sign…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096504] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi

We investigate the localization transition of interacting particles in a one-dimensional system with colored-noise disorder, where backward scattering processes are suppressed beyond a cutoff. Employing two complementary renormalization group procedures, we derive the phase diagram and reveal a sign…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096504] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Localization Transition for Interacting Quantum Particles in Colored-Noise Disorder
item.dc:creator: Giacomo Morpurgo, Laurent Sanchez-Palencia, and Thierry Giamarchi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Pressure-Induced 18 K Superconductivity and Two Superconducting Phases in ${\text{CuIr}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{4}$
item.description:
Author(s): Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding

CuIr2S4 becomes a bulk superconductor with Tc of 18.2 K under pressure, a new record for spinels, and splits into two distinct superconducting phases that coexist over a broad window.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096505] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding

CuIr2S4 becomes a bulk superconductor with Tc of 18.2 K under pressure, a new record for spinels, and splits into two distinct superconducting phases that coexist over a broad window.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096505] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-Induced 18 K Superconductivity and Two Superconducting Phases in ${\text{CuIr}}_{2}{\mathrm{S}}_{4}$
item.dc:creator: Bijuan Chen, Yuhao Gu, Dong Wang, Dexi Shao, Wen Deng, Xin Han, Meiling Jin, Jing Song, Yu Zeng, Hirofumi Ishii, Yen-Fa Liao, Dongzhou Zhang, Jianbo Zhang, Youwen Long, Jinlong Zhu, Liuxiang Yang, Hong Xiao, Jia-cai Nie, Youguo Shi, Changqing Jin, Jiangping Hu, Ho-kwang Mao, and Yang Ding
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Universal Chern Model on Arbitrary Triangulations
item.description:
Author(s): Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan

Given a triangulation of a closed orientable surface, we place single-mode resonators or single-orbital artificial atoms at its vertices, edges, and facets, and we devise near-neighbor hopping terms derived from the boundary and Poincaré duality maps of the simplicial complex of the triangulation. R…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096602] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan

Given a triangulation of a closed orientable surface, we place single-mode resonators or single-orbital artificial atoms at its vertices, edges, and facets, and we devise near-neighbor hopping terms derived from the boundary and Poincaré duality maps of the simplicial complex of the triangulation. R…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096602] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Chern Model on Arbitrary Triangulations
item.dc:creator: Nigel Higson and Emil Prodan

item.title: Quantum Oscillations of Nonlinear Electrical Transport in a Topological Dirac Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Vijaysankar Kalappattil, Chuanpu Liu, Zhijie Chen, Vipul Sharma, Kai Liu, Jinke Tang, Steven S.-L. Zhang, and Mingzhong Wu

Quantum oscillations in nonlinear electrical transport reveal the geometry and spin orientation of the Fermi surface in the Dirac semimetal α-Sn.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096603] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vijaysankar Kalappattil, Chuanpu Liu, Zhijie Chen, Vipul Sharma, Kai Liu, Jinke Tang, Steven S.-L. Zhang, and Mingzhong Wu

Quantum oscillations in nonlinear electrical transport reveal the geometry and spin orientation of the Fermi surface in the Dirac semimetal α-Sn.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096603] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Oscillations of Nonlinear Electrical Transport in a Topological Dirac Semimetal

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Diameter-Controlled High-Order Vortex States and Magnon Hybridization in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ Nanotubes
item.description:
Author(s): Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu

Curved magnets offer a rich phase diagram and hold great promise for next-generation spintronic technologies. This Letter establishes the paramount significance of high-order vortex states (e.g., $3φ$ with winding number $n≥2$) in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ nanotubes, which uniquely enable magnonic functi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096703] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu

Curved magnets offer a rich phase diagram and hold great promise for next-generation spintronic technologies. This Letter establishes the paramount significance of high-order vortex states (e.g., 3φ with winding number n≥2) in VSe2 nanotubes, which uniquely enable magnonic functionalities fundamenta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096703] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Diameter-Controlled High-Order Vortex States and Magnon Hybridization in ${\mathrm{VSe}}_{2}$ Nanotubes
item.dc:creator: Jia-Wen Li, Xin-Wei Yi, Jin Zhang, Gang Su, and Bo Gu

item.title: Anderson Localization in a Two-Dimensional Metal
item.description:
Author(s): Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

A new layered material enabled researchers to document a dramatic change in metallic electron behavior as the material goes from 3D to 2D.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096401] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

A new layered material enabled researchers to document a dramatic change in metallic electron behavior as the material goes from 3D to 2D.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096401] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Anderson Localization in a Two-Dimensional Metal
item.dc:creator: Morgan Thinel, Taketo Handa, Christie S. Koay, Daniel G. Chica, Nicholas Olsen, Apoorv Jindal, JeongHeon Choe, Xavier Roy, Xiaoyang Zhu, and Abhay N. Pasupathy

item.title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.description:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

Consecutive topological phase transitions (TPTs) between strongly correlated electronic phases that differ simultaneously in symmetry breaking and topological order are of fundamental interest in condensed matter physics, yet, rarely realized experimentally. We report two consecutive electric-field–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096503] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric-Field-Tuned Consecutive Topological Phase Transitions between Distinct Correlated Insulators in Moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ Heterobilayer
item.dc:creator: Xumin Chang, Zui Tao, Bowen Shen, Wanghao Tian, Jenny Hu, Kateryna Pistunova, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Tony F. Heinz, Tingxin Li, Kin Fai Mak, Jie Shan, and Shengwei Jiang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Paired Parton Trial States for the Superfluid-Fractional Chern Insulator Transition
item.description:
Author(s): Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon

We consider a model of hard-core bosons on a lattice half-filling a Chern band such that the system has a continuous transition between a fractional Chern insulator (FCI) and a superfluid state (SF) depending on the bandwidth to bandspacing ratio. We construct a parton-inspired trial wave function A…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096601] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon

We consider a model of hard-core bosons on a lattice half-filling a Chern band such that the system has a continuous transition between a fractional Chern insulator (FCI) and a superfluid state (SF) depending on the bandwidth to bandspacing ratio. We construct a parton-inspired trial wave function A…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096601] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Paired Parton Trial States for the Superfluid-Fractional Chern Insulator Transition
item.dc:creator: Tevž Lotrič and Steven H. Simon

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the $5d$ iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the $1/3$-hole self-doped system ${…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kai Wang, Jun Yang, Chaoyang Kang, Weikang Wu, Wenka Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Yaomin Dai, and Bing Xu

The combination of strong spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions makes the 5d iridates a unique platform for realizing novel correlated electronic states. Here, utilizing infrared spectroscopy, we demonstrate that a robust Mott insulating state persists in the 1/3-hole self-doped system La3Ir3…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-Selective Spin-Orbit Mott Insulator in Fractional Valence Iridate ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ir}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{11}$

item.title: Magnetic Signature of Chiral Phonons Revealed by Neutron Spectroscopy in Ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{1.75}{\mathrm{Zn}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$
item.description:
Author(s): Song Bao, Junbo Liao, Zhentao Huang, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhen Ma, Bo Zhang, Shufan Cheng, Hao Xu, Zihang Song, Shuai Dong, Maofeng Wu, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Mitsutaka Nakamura, Tom Fennell, Dmitry Khalyavin, and Jinsheng Wen

Neutron scattering has provided a new and broader view of the twirling collective atomic vibrations in a magnetic crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Song Bao, Junbo Liao, Zhentao Huang, Yanyan Shangguan, Zhen Ma, Bo Zhang, Shufan Cheng, Hao Xu, Zihang Song, Shuai Dong, Maofeng Wu, Ryoichi Kajimoto, Mitsutaka Nakamura, Tom Fennell, Dmitry Khalyavin, and Jinsheng Wen

Neutron scattering has provided a new and broader view of the twirling collective atomic vibrations in a magnetic crystal.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic Signature of Chiral Phonons Revealed by Neutron Spectroscopy in Ferrimagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{1.75}{\mathrm{Zn}}_{0.25}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantization of Spin Circular Photogalvanic Effect in Altermagnetic Weyl Semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami

We theoretically predict a spin-current analog of the quantized circular photogalvanic effect in Weyl semimetals. This phenomenon is forbidden in antiferromagnets by symmetry but uniquely allowed in altermagnets, highlighting a novel and intrinsic characteristic of altermagnetism. To systematically …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096701] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami

We theoretically predict a spin-current analog of the quantized circular photogalvanic effect in Weyl semimetals. This phenomenon is forbidden in antiferromagnets by symmetry but uniquely allowed in altermagnets, highlighting a novel and intrinsic characteristic of altermagnetism. To systematically …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096701] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantization of Spin Circular Photogalvanic Effect in Altermagnetic Weyl Semimetals
item.dc:creator: Hiroki Yoshida, Jan Priessnitz, Libor Šmejkal, and Shuichi Murakami
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Deterministic Switching of the Néel Vector by Asymmetric Spin Torque
item.description:
Author(s): Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao

Néel vector, the order parameter of collinear antiferromagnets, serves as a state variable in associated antiferromagnetic (AFM) spintronic devices to encode information. A deterministic switching of Néel vector is crucial for the write-in operation, which, however, remains a challenging problem in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096702] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao

Néel vector, the order parameter of collinear antiferromagnets, serves as a state variable in associated antiferromagnetic (AFM) spintronic devices to encode information. A deterministic switching of Néel vector is crucial for the write-in operation, which, however, remains a challenging problem in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096702] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Deterministic Switching of the Néel Vector by Asymmetric Spin Torque
item.dc:creator: Shui-Sen Zhang, Zi-An Wang, Bo Li, Wen-Jian Lu, Mingliang Tian, Yu-Ping Sun, Haifeng Du, and Ding-Fu Shao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ultrafast One-Dimensional Peierls-Distortion Dynamics in $1{\mathrm{T}}^{′}\text{−}\mathrm{Re}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ Revealed by 4D Electron Microscopy
item.description:
Author(s): Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu

Rhenium disulfide (${\mathrm{ReS}}_{2}$), a prototypical 2D semiconductor with an anisotropic ${1\mathrm{T}}^{′}$ structure due to the pronounced Peierls distortion, has demonstrated great potential for polarization-dependent optoelectronics and photonics. Here, we report an ultrafast phase transiti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096901] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu

Rhenium disulfide (ReS2), a prototypical 2D semiconductor with an anisotropic 1T′ structure due to the pronounced Peierls distortion, has demonstrated great potential for polarization-dependent optoelectronics and photonics. Here, we report an ultrafast phase transition occurring within 1 ps, accomp…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096901] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrafast One-Dimensional Peierls-Distortion Dynamics in $1{\mathrm{T}}^{′}\text{−}\mathrm{Re}{\mathrm{S}}_{2}$ Revealed by 4D Electron Microscopy
item.dc:creator: Jingchao Liu, Shaozheng Ji, Lifu Zhang, Yiping Jiao, Jiangteng Guo, Cuntao Gao, Fang Liu, Shibin Deng, Xuewei Cao, Zhenpeng Hu, Yimei Zhu, and Xuewen Fu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nonlinear Terahertz Electroluminescence from Dirac-Landau Polaritons
item.description:
Author(s): B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe

We report Dirac–Landau polaritons observed by terahertz (THz) magnetoreflectivity spectroscopy, demonstrating strong coupling between cyclotron transitions of two-dimensional Dirac fermions in HgTe quantum wells and optical cavity modes. Under pulsed electrical injection we observe efficient nonline…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096902] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe

We report Dirac–Landau polaritons observed by terahertz (THz) magnetoreflectivity spectroscopy, demonstrating strong coupling between cyclotron transitions of two-dimensional Dirac fermions in HgTe quantum wells and optical cavity modes. Under pulsed electrical injection we observe efficient nonline…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 096902] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Nonlinear Terahertz Electroluminescence from Dirac-Landau Polaritons
item.dc:creator: B. Benhamou-Bui, C. Consejo, S. S. Krishtopenko, S. Ruffenach, C. Bray, J. Torres, J. Dzian, F. Le Mardelé, A. Pagot, X. Baudry, S. V. Morozov, N. N. Mikhailov, S. A. Dvoretskii, B. Jouault, P. Ballet, M. Orlita, C. Ciuti, and F. Teppe
